title: Runaway/Homeless Youth Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > Runaway/Homeless Youth Counseling 10 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY BASIC CENTER BEND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline, short and long term shelter, case management and advocacy, crisis intervention, short term counseling, and mediation services between youth and their families. 2125 NE Daggett Lane Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Runaway and at-risk youth ages 11-17 Hours: Shelter and Crisis Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Other service times may vary. Email (541) 382-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides street outreach and individual case management for youth and young adults. Offers support, case management, and goal-setting tools to help identify and work towards future goals. Financial help and referrals to obtain interview clothes, laundry, obtain a birth certificate, Social Security card, ID, CPR/First Aid, or Food Handlers certification. Family mediation is available for families needing to work through a specific conflict. 225 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Ages 10-24. Individuals from outside the service area will not be turned away. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday by appointment Email (800) 901-2904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES YOUTH SERVICE CENTER AND DROP IN OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with street outreach and individual case management. Offers support, resources, referral help, and physical items. Case management and goal setting can be set up to help an individual name and work towards future goals. Family mediation is also available for families needing to work through a specific conflict. Financial help and referrals for youth needing to do laundry, obtain interview clothes, or obtain a birth certificate, Social Security card, ID, CPR/First Aid, or Food Handlers certification is also available. 1025 Pacific Boulevard SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 10-24. Individuals from outside the service area will not be turned away. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday by appointment Email (800) 901-2904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with laundry facilities, phone use, temporary mailing address, showers, and daily meals. Also provides street outreach, case management, mental health counseling, education, and assistance finding housing. Eligible youth will be added to a central housing referral list for all programs they qualify for. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: -Drop-in Center: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm -Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am-9:30am -Lunch: 7 days per week noon-1pm -Snack: 7 days per week 3pm-3:30pm -Dinner: 7 days per week 4pm-5pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HOMELESS YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps youth build positive relationships with adults and role models to improve their stability. Connects them with support services, including case management, cultural resources, mentors, mental health and addiction services, education, job assistance, as well as on-site food, clothing, and shelter resources. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are currently experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (503) 288-8177 326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD EUGENE MAIN OFFICE HUMAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Case managers provide comprehensive, wraparound human and social services. Staff intervene in crisis situations with direct assistance, classes, referrals, and advocacy. Offer assistance to women and children in distress, and a safe place for runaway teenagers. 195 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-6pm Email (541) 687-2667 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EMERGENCY SERVICES HEARTS WITH A MISSION YOUTH SHELTER JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HOMELESS YOUTH SERVICES PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD HUMAN SERVICES
